summary.scholar: On January 20, 2021, Joe Biden was inaugurated as the 46th President, and Kamala Harris was sworn in as Vice President — becoming the first woman, first Black person, and first person of South Asian descent to hold the office. The inauguration took place under unprecedented security following the January 6 Capitol breach, with the National Mall closed to the public.
summary.explorer: On January 20, 2021, Joe Biden became the 46th President. But the REALLY historic part was Kamala Harris — she became the first woman, first Black person, and first South Asian person to be Vice President! The inauguration had extra-heavy security because of what had happened at the Capitol just two weeks earlier.

significance.scholar: Harris's inauguration as Vice President represented a historic milestone in American representation, breaking barriers that had stood for over 230 years.
significance.explorer: Kamala Harris made history by becoming the first woman and first person of color to be Vice President. That's a really big deal!

sources.citation: Miller Center: U.S. Presidents and Key Events
